cards.title: Why Your Adult Child May Feel Pressured by You
cards.body: A parent's devotion to their adult son masks underlying pressure that affects their relationship. Reflecting on your own ambivalence about parenthood may reveal uncomfortable truths about what you expect from him.

cards.title: Managing Parental Anxiety on Family Holidays
cards.body: Chronic worry can overshadow family vacations, making you tense while others relax. Facing real emergencies head-on taught this parent that they're more resilient than their anxiety suggests.

cards.title: Breaking Dysfunctional Family Patterns in Adulthood
cards.body: Children of narcissistic or avoidant parents often replicate unhealthy relationship dynamics. Recognizing these patterns requires intentional work to understand what healthy relationships actually look like.

cards.title: Only Children Aren't Weird or Selfish
cards.body: Persistent stereotypes paint only children as maladjusted and their parents as selfish, but research doesn't support these myths. Single-child families are increasingly common and their children thrive.

cards.title: Navigating Blended Family Relationships After Divorce
cards.body: When a parent remarries, adult children face pressure to include the new partner in family life. Balancing fairness with relationship preservation requires honest conversations about boundaries.

cards.title: Performance Anxiety in Children: Healing the Critical Parent Within
cards.body: Stage fright often stems from internalized critical voices from childhood. Parents can help break this cycle by examining their own perfectionism and providing genuine reassurance.

cards.title: Understanding Pathological Demand Avoidance in Children
cards.body: PDA-linked patterns in children aren't permanent and can improve with the right family support and treatment. Gentle reshaping of these patterns strengthens parent-child relationships.

cards.title: Why Childhood Chaos Can Feel Safer Than Calm
cards.body: Adults who grew up in unstable homes may unconsciously seek chaos to feel grounded. Understanding this pattern helps break the cycle with your own children.

cards.title: Teaching Boys About Fertility From Early Age
cards.body: Many men only consider fertility when problems arise because society rarely addresses it during childhood. Changing these conversations early shapes healthier attitudes toward reproductive responsibility.

cards.title: Australia Tightens Social Media Rules for Minors
cards.body: Children under 16 are now banned from major social media platforms in Australia, with doubled penalties for breaching platforms. This legislation represents a significant shift in protecting young users.

cards.title: Phone Addiction Treatment for Teens Is Growing
cards.body: Addiction treatment centers report rising numbers of teenagers seeking help for excessive phone use. Parents should recognize warning signs of compulsive digital behavior affecting sleep and wellbeing.
